included: stage construction from an already-created NamespaceSource
prim path lookup on populated stages

model.construction_budget: Stage construction should scale with the number of source scene objects and properties that are reachable after mask and active filtering, not with the number of unrelated masked-out specs.
model.lookup_budget: Prim lookup by path should use a populated index or equivalent structure, not a full traversal scan per query.
model.ordering_budget: Child/property ordering should be computed once per population or cached lazily per prim, not resorted from raw strings on every query.
model.memory_budget: The populated stage should retain path/token/field identity handles from provider capabilities where possible and avoid duplicating full path strings or adapter JSON field maps for every query.

populate.expected_complexity: O(P + E) where P is populated prim count and E is exposed property count for populated prims, after source construction and excluding parser I/O.

prim_lookup.expected_complexity: Amortized O(1) or O(log P) by path identity, not O(P) traversal scan.

children.expected_complexity: O(C) to return C children, with ordering already cached or computed from source child order plus sparse primOrder.

properties.expected_complexity: O(R) to return R properties, with path-element sorting/propertyOrder application cached or bounded to property count.

mask_filtering.expected_complexity: Population mask checks should be based on path identity/prefix structure and should not repeatedly rescan complete path strings for every source spec.

shape_gates: masked population cost should track populated ancestors and requested subtrees, not total source specs
inactive-pruned population should not allocate stage nodes for descendants of inactive prims
lookup timing should stay substantially flatter than full traversal timing as prim count grows
measurement.note: Concrete machine-specific thresholds should live in benchmark target files once adapters exist. This contract defines the measurement model and optimization direction.
